description: Add monthly PDF report generation using reportlab. Users can request a PDF report via a button, or it can be generated at month end. PDF includes: house name, utility type, month, start/end readings, usage amount, payment sum, date.
steps: Create pdf_report.py module in project root. It should have a function generate_monthly_pdf(house_name, utility_type, month_year, start_reading, end_reading, usage, cost, date_generated, lang='uz') that returns a BytesIO buffer with the PDF. Use reportlab.lib.pagesizes, reportlab.platypus (SimpleDocTemplate, Table, TableStyle, Paragraph), reportlab.lib.styles, reportlab.lib.colors. The PDF should have a title, a table with the data, and proper formatting.
Add database function in database.py: get_monthly_report_data(telegram_id, house_id, utility_type, year, month) that returns the first and last readings of a given month for calculating usage.
Add a 'PDF hisobot' / 'PDF otchet' button to the main keyboard (get_main_kb function). Add it as a new row in the keyboard.

Add handler in main.py for the PDF report button. When pressed: show user their houses, let them select house and utility type, then generate PDF for the current or previous month. Send the PDF as a document using BufferedInputFile.
Integrate with i18n: use get_text() for all PDF report related messages and the PDF content labels (headers, column names).
Add scheduled job or integrate with existing month-end reminder to optionally auto-generate and send PDF reports to users who have readings for the past month.
acceptance_criteria: PDF contains: house name, utility type, month, start reading, end reading, usage, cost, date
Main keyboard has PDF report button
Handler allows selecting house and utility for PDF generation
PDF is sent as a document to the user
All .py files pass Python syntax validation
verification: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('pdf_report.py').read()); ast.parse(open('main.py').read()); ast.parse(open('database.py').read()); print('All syntax OK')"
grep -q 'generate_monthly_pdf' pdf_report.py && echo 'PDF function exists' || echo 'MISSING PDF function'
grep -q 'pdf' main.py && echo 'PDF handler in main.py' || echo 'MISSING PDF handler'
grep -q 'get_monthly_report_data' database.py && echo 'Report data function exists' || echo 'MISSING report data function'
findings: All verification checks pass. PDF report feature includes: pdf_report.py with generate_monthly_pdf(), get_monthly_report_data() in database.py, PDF button in main keyboard, full handler flow (house -> utility -> month selection -> PDF generation), i18n keys in both uz/ru locales, and auto-send scheduled job at month start (day=1, hour=10). The PdfReportForm FSM class was added but callbacks are used instead of FSM state flow for simpler interaction.
